type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: SRU operated for all of 2015.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Phase 2B was commissioned in mid-2013, resulting in the addition of several equipment units to the MEG Christina Lake Regional Project. Four once through steam generators (OTSGs) and an HP and LP flare stack were installed. Also, two glycol heaters and two diesel fired emergency generators were added to meet additional heating demands. This resulted in greater than 10% emissions of NOx compared to the previous year.

comments.comment: The increase in SO2 emissions is due to 1. annual production increase and 2. Implementation of a process optimization strategy that  allows higher H2S concentration gas to be combusted while still achieving the overall sulphur recovery target. MEG operated within it's daily SO2 limits for all for 2018.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Updated flare SO2 calculation methodology to more accurately represent flare and non-flare events. Also, Phase 1 steam generator now fed only with sweet natural gas. Sulphur content in diesel generators was assumed to be 100%. This was updated to 0.015% and the diesel generator calculations were updated accordingly. These changes resulted in a >10% decrease in overall stack SO2 emissions from last year.
